public_goals: Get the reimbursement discussion moving without having to re-explain every record from the beginning.
Avoid being treated as if handling the paperwork means acting in bad faith.
Agree on a clearer document-sharing and summary process if that is what is needed.
private_concerns: Feels frustrated that administrative competence is being turned into a reason to doubt them.
Does not fully appreciate how intimidating the information gap can feel.
Wants closure but would rather slow down than have the process later called unfair.
red_lines: Will not accept being treated as automatically untrustworthy for having handled the paperwork.
Will not keep revisiting the same expense categories if a clearer process can be built instead.
interest_profile: A shared records process that stops repeated confusion
emotional_triggers: being accused of hiding information by default
having basic records questions turn into character judgments
disclosure_tendencies: provides details quickly but may overestimate how understandable they sound
more willing to share documents than to slow down verbally
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes sharper if competence is framed as bad faith
cooperates more readily when the process distinguishes document access from blame
starting_positions: Primary position: the records can be shared, but the conversation needs more structure so it does not turn into the same confusion repeatedly.
Secondary position: written summaries and a shared review step are better than forcing reimbursement conclusions now.
likely_openings: I am not trying to hide anything, but we need a cleaner way to review this than arguing line by line in the moment.
If the records need to be shared more clearly, then let us do that before pretending the numbers are settled.
